key_points: Matter is made up of tiny particles that are constantly moving
The kinetic energy of particles increases with increase in temperature
Diffusion is the process of intermixing of particles due to their motion
Solids have fixed shape and volume because their particles are tightly packed
Liquids have fixed volume but no fixed shape because particles can move freely
Gases have neither fixed shape nor fixed volume due to large spaces between particles
Intermolecular forces of attraction are strongest in solids and weakest in gases
Change of state occurs when energy is absorbed or released by particles
examples: Smell of incense stick spreading in a room shows diffusion in gases
Ink spreading in water demonstrates diffusion in liquids
Ice melting into water when heated is an example of melting
Water changing into steam on heating shows boiling or vaporization
Steam changing into water droplets shows condensation
formulas: Melting point: temperature at which solid changes into liquid
Boiling point: temperature at which liquid changes into gas
exam_tips: Understand particle arrangement in solids, liquids and gases
Explain diffusion with daily life examples
Remember the processes of change of state: melting, freezing, boiling, condensation and sublimation
Use Kelvin scale when writing temperature in scientific form
Write differences between solids, liquids and gases clearly in exams
